title: golemic submit-review Subcommand
summary: Review submission atomically coupled with event logging. golemic submit-review --verdict approved|changes_requested --body <text> --pr <n> reads context from environment, validates the verdict, submits the review via gh pr review, and writes a review_submitted event. If gh fails, no event is written and exit != 0.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Reviewer agent (LLM) running inside the reviewer worktree
stakeholder_intent.goal: Submit a formal GitHub review and atomically record the verdict in the event log
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Single atomic action that couples the GitHub review submission with the event log entry, ensuring the verdict is always recorded. The only authoritative channel for verdicts.
stakeholder_intent.trigger: Reviewer agent finishes analysis and calls golemic submit-review --verdict approved --body 'LGTM' --pr 123
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A GitHub review is submitted (approve or request changes) and a review_submitted event is written to the event log

in_scope: Reads GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG from environment (I1.1)
Verdict validation: only 'approved' and 'changes_requested' are accepted
gh pr review <n> --approve for approved verdict
gh pr review <n> --request-changes --body <text> for changes_requested verdict
Writes review_submitted event with verdict, body, prNumber
If gh fails → no event written, exit != 0
Invalid verdict → exit != 0 without gh call
out_of_scope: Loop outcome decisions based on verdict

preconditions: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, and GH_TOKEN (reviewer token) are set in the environment by the runner
A PR exists for the current run

business_rules.rule: The --verdict flag accepts only exactly 'approved' or 'changes_requested' (case-sensitive)
business_rules.outcome: Invalid verdict → error message listing valid values, exit != 0, no gh call

business_rules.rule: For verdict approved, gh pr review <n> --approve is called
For verdict changes_requested, gh pr review <n> --request-changes --body <text> is called
If gh pr review fails (non-zero exit), no review_submitted event is written
business_rules.applies_when: gh pr review exits with non-zero code
business_rules.outcome: Error message includes gh stderr, exit code != 0, no event written

business_rules.rule: Invalid verdict results in exit != 0 without any gh call
business_rules.outcome: Error message, exit != 0, no gh call, no event

then.result: Error: invalid verdict, exit != 0, no gh call
Error: gh failure, exit != 0, no event

errors.message: Invalid verdict: must be 'approved' or 'changes_requested', got: <value>

integration_contracts.transport: gh pr review via os/exec (inherits GH_TOKEN from environment)
integration_contracts.operation: gh pr review <n> --approve or gh pr review <n> --request-changes --body <text>
integration_contracts.idempotency: Not fully idempotent; repeated calls with same verdict may be accepted by GitHub but only the first counts as a new review
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → GH_REVIEW_FAILED with gh stderr

changes.value_source: gh pr review command (approve or request-changes)
review_submitted event with verdict, body, prNumber

security.authentication: GH_TOKEN from environment (reviewer token); inherited from parent process
authorization_rules: The reviewer token must have write access to submit reviews on the host repo
audit_requirements: review_submitted event records verdict, body, and PR number

architecture_constraints: Must validate verdict before any gh call (fail-fast)
Must NOT write event if gh fails
Must use the existing subcommand dispatch in main.go
allowed_changes: Add submit-review subcommand handler to main.go
forbidden_changes: Do not accept verdict values other than approved and changes_requested
Do not write event on gh failure
dependencies: Standard library: os/exec, os, flag, fmt, strings
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded CLI subcommand; no concurrency concerns

acceptance_scenarios.title: Approved verdict calls gh with --approve and writes event
given: A fake gh that returns success
then: review_submitted event is written with verdict=approved, body='LGTM', prNumber=123

acceptance_scenarios.title: Changes_requested verdict calls gh with --request-changes and writes event
given: A fake gh that returns success
when: golemic submit-review --verdict changes_requested --body 'Fix NPE' --pr 123 is invoked
then: gh pr review 123 --request-changes --body 'Fix NPE' is called
review_submitted event is written with verdict=changes_requested

acceptance_scenarios.title: Invalid verdict returns error without gh call
then: Error message mentions valid verdict values

acceptance_scenarios.title: gh failure results in no event and non-zero exit
given: A fake gh that returns non-zero exit

test_cases: Invalid verdict returns error without gh call
gh failure returns error without event
Missing env vars return error before gh call
Event contains correct verdict, body, prNumber
non_functional_requirements: Verdict validation is case-sensitive (only lowercase approved/changes_requested)
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
submit-review subcommand is wired into main.go dispatch

decision_log.decision: Verdict accepts only exactly 'approved' and 'changes_requested' (matching GitHub API terminology)
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Verdikt-Validierung (nur die zwei Werte)' and §2.6: 'verdict ∈ {approved, changes_requested}'

decision_log.decision: approved → gh pr review --approve; changes_requested → gh pr review --request-changes --body
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'gh pr review <n> --approve bzw. --request-changes --body …'

decision_log.decision: submit-review subcommand uses the existing dispatch in cmd/golemic/main.go
decision_log.rationale: main.go already lists submit-review in knownCommands

codebase_evidence.finding: submit-review is already listed as a known command with description 'Submit a review (not implemented)' and dispatches to 'not implemented' path
